Main finding: Depth mainly affects spread, complexity, deformation position, and phase
Interpretation: Depth is weaker than size and condition-dependent

Main finding: Depth classification relies more on deformation-position / shape-contrast / spread than amplitude
Interpretation: Structured cues exist in tactile data

Main finding: Unified model latent features recover depth-related concepts better than size-only probe
Interpretation: Model encodes depth-related structure beyond size identity

Main finding: Shape and tabular branches are critical
Interpretation: Final model is not relying on a single cue

Main finding: Model still depends most on peak neighborhood
Interpretation: Temporal strategy does not fully match the hypothesized loading/release dominance

Main finding: Deeper sample receives higher p_deep in most matched pairs
Interpretation: Model is not only using peak amplitude
Limitation: Evidence is moderate rather than absolute
